title: Alumni Council | The Webb Schools | Private Boarding & Day School
content:
The Alumni Council is the primary volunteer leadership group for alumni of The Webb Schools. We consist of 40 men and women, who represent seven decades and many regions worldwide. The Council’s goals are to connect alumni to Webb, Webb to the alumni body, and alumni with each other.
The Alumni Council meets four times a year to plan programs and activities which benefit the Webb community. These include:
Career Evening
– alumni guest speakers share their career experiences with sophomores
Senior Dinner
– annual event to welcome the newest class of alumni to our alumni family
Regional alumni events
include cultural and sporting activities and networking opportunities
WebbWorks
online alumni community for career assistance and mentoring
Affinity Groups
for Women of Webb (alumnae networking), Webb15 (young alumni group for classes of the past 15 years), and Latinos of Webb.
Archival projects
in support of our Centennial Celebration in 2022 and beyond. These include the Teachers of Webb Survey and the Oral History Video Project.
If you would like to contact the Alumni Council with a question or comment, please email
alumni@webb.org
or log in to the Online Community.
Alumni Council Members
List of 19 items.
Coleen Martinez '86, President
Coleen Martinez is currently President of the Alumni Council at Webb. She returned as a Member at Large in 2024 after previously serving from 2001-2022. During those 21 years, Coleen remained an active participant at various capacities in the Webb community, serving on the Women of Webb affinity group and was a constant fixture at alumni events in the area. She has also been a Class Agent for many years and returned to Webb in 2017 as the VWS commencement speaker. In 2019, Coleen also participated at the Alumnae Pioneers Panel and spoke about the early days of VWS and the challenges that the pioneer girls encountered. During her time at Webb, Coleen was an active member of the community, participating in drama, music, swimming, peccary trips, and various clubs. Coleen received a B.A. in Spanish from Pitzer College and a M.A. in education from the Claremont Graduate University before pursuing her career as an elementary school teacher. She has been an elementary school teacher for over 30 years and currently is a second-grade teacher at Foothill Country Day School in Claremont where she has been teaching for the last two decades.
David Ivey-Soto  '82, Vice President
David Ivey-Soto ’82 is the current National Treasurer and a former National Secretary of the American Culinary Federation serving on its Board of Directors. David Ivey-Soto earned his AOS from the Culinary Institute of America where he was on the Dean’s List, was the Student Council President, and was Sommelier de Vin. He was honored to be selected as a Fellow of the Culinary Institute of America where he worked with chefs Clark and Canner in the Fish Kitchen and with Mr. Schmitter in the American Bounty Dining Room. He has earned Certified Executive Chef (CEC®) and Certified Culinary Administration (CCA®) from the American Culinary Federation. In 2005 he earned an MBA from the College of William & Mary. David is very active in his Webb class; serving as a reunion chair, serving as a regional representative on the Alumni Council, and hosting events in D.C. Member since 2013.
Kathy Fredrich '02, Immediate Past President
Kathy Fredrich ’02 graduated from UC San Diego with a B.S. in Physiology & Neuroscience and a B.A. in Psychology. She is currently a marketing consultant for Crayola, bringing over 15 years of experience in brand strategy and product development within the consumer packaged goods space — particularly in the pet and toy industries. She previously held the role of Associate Brand Manager, Global Brand Development & Marketing at Hasbro, and spent nearly a decade at Natural Balance Pet Foods. Kathy has remained an active alumna since graduating — serving as a reunion chair, career evening speaker, attending alumni events and helping to build connections throughout the alumni community. Since 2006, she has also volunteered as a Los Angeles Regional Club Leader for UC San Diego’s Alumni Association, including serving as a past club president. While at Webb, she was a dorm counselor at North Hutch and a member of the volleyball and basketball teams.
Kathy served as President of the Alumni Council from 2021 to 2025 and has been a member since 2013.
Jason Brooks '99
Jason Brooks ’99  is the Founder of harkness.ai, which shows how individual interactions affect meeting dynamics and delivers feedback that prompts users to reflect on their participation habits. He has been a speaker at Sophomore Career Evening and now joins the Alumni Council. Previously, he was the head of Upper School at The Episcopal School of LA. While at Webb, Jason was involved in basketball, track and football. Jason has a long list of higher education degrees including degrees from Trinity University, Valparaiso University and Harvard University. Jason lives in Los Angeles with his wife, Christa. Member since 2022.
Jeff Cannon '84
Jeff Cannon is a flight attendant with Southwest Airlines and the owner of The Cannon Group, a consulting firm providing technology services. He earned a B.F.A. in comparative history of ideas from the University of Washington and an associate of arts degree in economics from Seattle Central College. Jeff has remained an active alumnus, serving as a class agent from 2006 to 2013 and as reunion chair for his 25th reunion in 2009. He continues to attend alumni events and Alumni Weekend to stay connected with the Webb community. He currently resides in Chicago, Illinois.
Jeff has been a member of the Alumni Council since 2025.
Ariel Fan '10
Ariel Fan ’10 is proud to have started her environmental advocacy career during her time at Webb as the founder of Project Earth on the Service Council — organizing beach cleanups, plastic bag recycling campaigns at the dining hall and advocating to ban Kleenex tissues on campus due to unsustainable foresting practices. Today, as the founder and CEO of GreenWealth Energy Partners, a turnkey developer of EV charging and LED lighting solutions, Ariel is helping government and real estate clients develop infrastructure to transition to the clean energy future. GreenWealth is proud to be the first woman and minority-owned energy company certified in the state of CA — advocating for diversity and equity in today’s new clean energy economy. Ariel received her degree in environmental policy, economics and art history from Barnard College of Columbia University. Member since 2020.
Jordan Fang '19
Jordan Fang earned his bachelor’s degree from Occidental College, where he double majored in economics and diplomacy & world affairs. He currently leads AI and automation strategy at Articulate Global and also runs his own consultancy focused on helping organizations implement AI-driven solutions to improve efficiency and scale operations.
Jordan remains closely involved with the Webb alumni community, regularly attending events, serving as reunion chair for the Class of 2019’s 5-year reunion, and contributing ideas and strategies for alumni networking programs. While at Webb, he participated in honors orchestra, basketball, and water polo, and also served as a student coach for the JV water polo team. He currently resides in San Diego, California.
Jordan has been a member of the Alumni Council since 2025.
Christine French '90
Christine French ’90 is a biologist and technical writer for Environmental Science Associates, focusing primarily on construction compliance for infrastructure projects. Previously, she had been a research analyst for Grassroots Health where she co-authored several papers on the relationship of Vitamin D to several health conditions and spent seven years at the UC Center for Water Resources working on agricultural water management. She has been a reunion chair many times and often attends regional events. She’s also been a speaker at Sophomore/Junior Career Evening. At Webb, Christine was a student counselor. Christine attended UC Santa Barbara and UC Riverside. Christine lives in Redlands with her husband, Ross.
Christine has been an Alumni Council member since 2022.
Jim Hawkins '70
Jim Hawkins ’70 is very engaged within his class, and he took a great interest in achieving the DEI match from the E.E. Ford Foundation. Jim has a long career in commercial real estate and is a senior associate at Lee & Associates Commercial Real Estate Services, Inc. in Orange. Jim achieved his B.A. from USC and his M.S. from UC Davis. He lives in Newport Beach with his wife, Betsy.
Jim has been an Alumni Council member since 2022.
Stephanie Ho '04
Stephanie Ho ’04 is the Senior Vice President of Operations at Media Res Studio in Los Angeles, CA. Previously, Stephanie spent nearly a decade at MGM Studios, rising to Vice President of TV Operations. She earned her MBA from UCLA Anderson and holds a B.S. in Business Management from Boston College, where she was an NCAA Division I Track & Field student-athlete. At Webb, Stephanie was deeply involved in student life, serving on ASB, and was a multi-sport varsity athlete, earning Athlete of the Year honors, while setting records for VWS Track & Field. Since graduating, she has remained a dedicated alumna — serving as a reunion chair, graduation commencement speaker, career evening speaker, class fundraising leader, and was the inaugural recipient of the Young Alumni Rising Star Award in 2014.
Stephanie returned to the Alumni Council in 2023 after completing two consecutive three-year terms from 2016–2022. She has been a member since 2016.
Faye Karnavy Sahai '86
Faye Karnavy Sahai ’86 has been a reunion chair for many of her reunions over the years, has been a class agent, and has often contributed content to the Daughters Strong and Webb 1986 Facebook groups. Faye is a graduate of Claremont McKenna College and UCLA. While at Webb, Faye was in the 3rd class of girls, VWS Chorus, Student Council, honor committee, co-editor in chief of El Espejo and tennis and track team. Faye spent 30 years working in health care, financial institutions and insuretech and is currently the managing director, board member, and strategic advisor of the Exbourne Group. She lives in the Bay Area with her husband, Avanish and has two kids.
Faye has been an Alumni Council member since 2022.
Doug Lee '96
Doug Lee earned his bachelor’s degree in business administration and finance from Carnegie Mellon University. He currently works as a real estate broker with Compass and as a real estate coach with Forward Coaching, bringing over 25 years of experience in both commercial and residential properties. Since graduating from Webb, Doug has remained deeply connected to the community by attending alumni events, reconnecting with fellow Webbies around the world, and returning to campus to speak with students about life after Webb, including most recently at Junior Career Evening 2025. He also served as reunion chair for the Class of 1996’s 15th reunion. While a student at Webb, Doug was an active athlete, participating in basketball, baseball, cross country, football, golf, and track & field. He currently lives in Alhambra, California.
Doug has been a member of the Alumni Council since 2025.
Bassil Madanat '04
Bassil Madanat ’04 graduated from UC San Diego with a B.S. in Physiology & Neuroscience, and earned his J.D. from Pepperdine School of Law. A registered patent attorney, Bassil is the Founder and Principal Attorney at MAD IP, A Professional Law Corporation, where he handles a variety of commercial IP matters for start-ups and Fortune 500 companies alike. Before private practice, Bassil externed at the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of California, worked in Legal Affairs at Fox Broadcasting Company and clerked for The White House Office of Science & Technology Policy under President Barack Obama. While at Webb, Bassil was Day Student Representative, co-headed Junior Fountain and was active in Varsity sports, including soccer, track & field, and cross-country.
Bassil has been a member of Alumni Council since 2023.
Carly Paris '10
Carly Paris is Vice President, Investment Banker at Bank of America in Los Angeles. She earned an A.B. in economics from Princeton University and an M.B.A. from the Stephen M. Ross School of Business at the University of Michigan. Carly has remained a dedicated member of the Webb alumni community, serving as Regional Representative for New York from 2018 to 2023 and for Los Angeles in 2024, and volunteering with the Admission Office at the 2017 Inspiring Young Minds Fair. At Webb, she was a member of the Honor Cabinet, an Academic Outreach Tutor, president of the Black Student Union, and a member of the softball team. Now based in Los Angeles, Carly looks forward to continuing her involvement on the Alumni Council.
Jim Reese '67
Jim Reese ’67 was active in sports while at Webb, participating in football, basketball, and baseball. He attended Duke University and earned a B.S. degree in Mechanical Engineering.  Before retiring in 2019, he worked as a consulting engineer specializing in energy management and air emissions control in the commercial and industrial sectors. His energy experience ranges from electrical power generation to improving energy utilization at industrial and commercial facilities. For the past 16 years, he has interviewed high school seniors applying to Duke University for the Duke Admissions office. One of his hobbies is participating in high power model rocketry with his son David. Jim lives in Dana Point, CA with his wife Barbara.
Jim has been a member of the Alumni Council since 2024.
Juliana Romeo '17
Juliana Romeo lives in Washington, D.C. and is an Account Manager in the Enterprise Learning + Skills division at Pearson, the world’s leading learning company committed to creating vibrant and enriching learning experiences designed for real-life impact. Juliana continued her time in Claremont and attended Scripps College where she earned a B.A. in anthropology and media studies. During her time at Webb, she was an active leader on campus supporting new students as a peer counselor and helping build community for boarding students as a Chapel Council member. She also created a photography project titled "Humans of Webb" that highlighted the day-to-day experiences of Webb students. She is excited to join the Council and continue building and fostering the alumni community in DC and across the East Coast.
Juliana has been an Alumni Council member since 2024.
Nihar Shah '04
Nihar Shah ’04 is a graduate of Columbia University and NYU School of Law. He is the founder of Santa Bella Investments, LLC, a specialty finance firm focusing on alternative energy. While a day student at Webb, Nihar was a member of the varsity football team and student government. Since graduating, Nihar has spoken at Sophomore Career Night, participated on the reunion committee, and has been on the Alumni Council since 2020.
Eissa Villaseñor '98
Eissa Villasenor ’98 is a Supervisory HQ Refugee Officer at U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services. She has served as a class agent and was the chapel speaker during Alumni Weekend in 2018. She often attends events in her area and has served as a mentor during networking mixers for the school. At Webb, Eissa was a member of the Latino Alliance and was vice president of VWS student government. Eissa earned her B.A. at Mount Holyoke College and her J.D. at the University of Minnesota. Eissa lives in La Quinta.
Eissa has been an Alumni Council member since 2022.
Jim Wetrich '75
Jim Wetrich ’75 has been a class agent for many years and stays connected within his class. Jim is a graduate of USC, Tulane University and Emory University. Jim is the CEO of The Wetrich Group and provides executive outplacement, leadership development, coaching and executive search services to health care providers. He’s also a Board of Councilors member at The Carter Center and an adjunct professor of marketing at Texas Wesleyan University, among many other positions in the healthcare, biosciences and coaching industries. Jim lives in Southlake, Texas with his wife, Nancy. Member since 2022.
Alumni Council Regional Representatives
List of 13 items.
Kunal Shah ’97, Orange County, CA
Kunal Shah ’97 is a founding partner at PBS Engineers, with 6 offices in California and Texas, headquartered in San Dimas, CA. He has been a volunteer for many years, serving as a reunion chair, class agent, and a frequent guest speaker at Sophomore/Junior Career Evening. Kunal received his B.S. with honors from UC Irvine. He lives in Orange County with his wife and two children.
Kunal has been an Alumni Council member since 2013.
Whitney Wharton '05, San Diego, CA
Whitney Wharton ’05 received her B.A. in psychology from Georgetown University. She was formerly an assistant managing clerk for the law firm Willkie Farr & Gallagher LLP in Washington, D.C. Whitney and her husband relocated to Southern California in July 2013, and she started work as a Project Engineer with Dickinson Cameron Construction Co. in Carlsbad. She now works as a Project Manager at General Atomics, managing capital projects in the facilities division. She also runs her own business, Agaves of California, and is co-chair of the Alumni Council’s Women of Webb Affinity group. Whitney became vice president of the Alumni Council on July 1, 2017.
Whitney has been a member of Alumni Council since 2013.
Will Habos '04, San Francisco, CA
Will Habos ’04 recently completed his MBA at Wharton School of Business at the University of Pennsylvania. Previously, he worked in the investment banking field for Canaccord Genuity. He has a B.S. in finance and accounting from New York University’s Stern School of Business. Member since 2014.
Jessica Dholakia '07, San Francisco, CA
Jessica Dholakia ’07 is a senior manager at Taulia, Inc., a financial supply chain company that works with global brands. She has a B.A. in international relations from George Washington University and resides in San Francisco, CA. Member since 2013.
AJ Hess '12, New York, NY
AJ Hess, ’12 is a Multimedia Journalist covering economics, politics, and technology for outlets such as CNBC, Fast Company, and TIME. As a Senior Editor at TIME, Hess oversees the magazine's op-ed section. A LinkedIn Top Voice and Wharton Seminar Scholar, Hess earned a dual bachelor’s degree in media arts & sciences and political science from Wellesley College. While at Webb, Hess was a senior ambassador, day student prefect, and competed in the varsity soccer, varsity swim and dive, and varsity track and field teams.
AJ has been an Alumni Council member since 2021.
Alexis De La Rosa '11, Denver, CO
Alexis De La Rosa ‘11 attended Bryn Mawr College, where she earned a bachelor’s degree in psychology and Latin American, Latino, Iberian people and cultures. She earned her master’s degree from the University of Pennsylvania in higher education/higher education administration. She works for the University of Pennsylvania as an assistant director of development for undergraduate financial aid and serves as a member of the Diversity, Equity & Inclusion Action Committee. Alexis was a volleyball, water polo, and track & field athlete at Webb and participated in the Global Citizen Corps. Member since 2021.
David Ivey-Soto  '82, Washington, DC
David Ivey-Soto ’82 is the current National Treasurer and a former National Secretary of the American Culinary Federation serving on its Board of Directors. David Ivey-Soto earned his AOS from the Culinary Institute of America where he was on the Dean’s List, was the Student Council President, and was Sommelier de Vin. He was honored to be selected as a Fellow of the Culinary Institute of America where he worked with chefs Clark and Canner in the Fish Kitchen and with Mr. Schmitter in the American Bounty Dining Room. He has earned Certified Executive Chef (CEC®) and Certified Culinary Administration (CCA®) from the American Culinary Federation. In 2005 he earned an MBA from the College of William & Mary. David is very active in his Webb class; serving as a reunion chair, serving as a regional representative on the Alumni Council, and hosting events in D.C. Member since 2013.
Preston Clarke '98, Phoenix, AZ
Preston Clarke ’98 graduated from Wheaton College with a B.A. in economics and earned his MBA from the University of Arizona. He is a managing partner of Arizona Venture Consulting, which provides advisory services to private equity-backed, middle market, and early-stage companies. While at Webb, Preston was a dorm prefect and a member of the varsity soccer, swim, and water polo teams. He resides in Scottsdale, Arizona. On the council, Preston has been active in supporting the Webb15 Affinity group and Webb@Work internship program.
Preston has been a member of Alumni Council since 2009.
Keating Leung '98, Hong Kong
Keating Leung ’98, a University of Southern California graduate with a bachelor’s degree in business administration and computer science, resides in Hong Kong with his wife and two daughters. Passionate about social good, Keating founded Sunny Days Smile, a non-governmental organization providing critical dental care to low-income families, enhancing access to essential healthcare. He also leads an initiative supporting individuals with disabilities, establishing a Hong Kong-based home that delivers care, resources, and opportunities to empower this community. At Webb, Keating was the member of the varsity water polo, wrestling, and swim teams.
Keating has been a member of the Alumni Council since 2013.
Henry Xu '12, Greater Bay Area, China
Henry Xu ’12 graduated from NYU with a B.A. in economics, producing, and math. He currently is working on a startup company, Gifti, based in Guangzhou, China that helps businesses and organizations to procure holiday or recognition gifts for employees or customers. While at Webb, Henry was a Dorm Council Prefect, Student Ambassador, captain of the basketball team, and played volleyball.
Henry has been a member of the Alumni Council since 2023.
Joanne Goh '99, Shanghai, China
Joanne Goh ‘99 graduated from Cornell University with a MEng and B.S. in chemical engineering. A Hong Kong native, she resides in Shanghai and is a vice president and China market specialist at Citi. While at Webb, Joanne was a boarder, and she has particularly fond memories of dance, chemistry and French classes. She is proud to become the first alumni regional rep in Shanghai and cannot wait to share her Webb experiences and help promote Webb in the region. Member since 2018.
Chan Woo Sung '86, Seoul, South Korea
Chan Sung is a member of Webb class of 1986. After graduating from Webb, Chan attended University of California, Irvine where he earned a B.A. in political science in 1990. Chan then attended New York Law School, where he was a member of the Law Review. After graduating NYLS in 1993, he worked as a maritime lawyer for 10 years at a law firm where the mother of former Webb Head of Schools Susan Nelson worked for many years (although Chan never had the pleasure of working with her). Chan returned to Korea with his family in 2003 where he continued his legal career through several different companies including one of Korea’s leading law firms, an electronics company, a tobacco company, a large retailer, and an alcohol/ beverage company. Currently, Chan is the Senior Director of Legal at Nike Korea. Chan has been married to his wife Beatrice for over thirty years and they live in Seoul with his son, Curtis. His daughter Sabrina lives in New York after she graduated with a Master’s degree.
Chan has been a member of the Alumni Council since 2017.
Mabel Hsu '01, Taiwan
Mabel Hsu is now working as a consultant for family offices and based in Taipei, Taiwan. Previously, she was in charge of finance and investor relations for Cherubic Ventures, a venture capital focusing on partnering with early stage startups across US and China. She has over 15 years of investment banking experience from Morgan Stanley, Credit Suisse, and Mizuho Securities across Hong Kong, Singapore, and San Francisco. Mabel also worked in investor relations at Alibaba Group. She holds a B.S. in economics from University of Pennsylvania. While here at Webb, Mabel was a part of the Sinfonia music program and received the Sinfonia Scholarship Award as well as the Frances and J.E. Libaw Orchestra Director’s Award, both of which recognized her for her excellent musicianship and superior leadership while at Webb. Two of Mabel’s siblings also attended Webb and she hopes for her daughter to one day be able to attend Webb as well.
Mabel has been a member of the Alumni Council since 2024.
